description:
Jira: TBD (inherits from parent epic mutagen-wrd)

Advances: mutagen.mutation_pipeline, mutagen.coverage

Deliverable:
- New lib/mutagen_ex/application.ex implementing Application behaviour. One-for-one supervisor named MutagenEx.Supervisor whose only child is a named Task.Supervisor registered as MutagenEx.TaskSup.
- mix.exs: add `mod: {MutagenEx.Application, []}` to `application/0`. Do not change `extra_applications`.
- .spec/decisions/supervision_tree.md is already authored by /distill — verify it is on disk and unmodified; do not regenerate.

Verification:
- Tests to pass:
  - v1..v5 of mutagen.mutation_pipeline stay green (mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_test.exs, baseline_test.exs, mutation_runner_raise_test.exs, integration/c1_test.exs, integration/c2_test.exs)
  - v1..v4 of mutagen.coverage stay green (mix test test/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner_test.exs, ast_cache_test.exs)
  - TR-5 concrete check: mix test test/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver_test.exs test/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver_property_test.exs test/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner_test.exs green with the Application running
  - mix compile --warnings-as-errors green
- Behaviors to demonstrate:
  - Fresh `iex -S mix`: `Process.whereis(MutagenEx.TaskSup) |> is_pid()` returns true
  - Fresh `iex -S mix`: `Process.whereis(MutagenEx.Supervisor) |> is_pid()` returns true
  - `Application.ensure_all_started(:mutagen_ex)` returns `{:ok, _started}` (scenario mutagen.mutation_pipeline.s11 — verification deferred to S4 but the runtime behaviour must hold from S1)

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner/mutation_loop.ex (that is S2)
- lib/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner.ex (that is S3)
- test/mutagen_ex/supervision_test.exs (that is S4)
- README.md, CHANGELOG.md (that is S5)
- .spec/specs/mutation_pipeline.spec.md, .spec/specs/coverage.spec.md (already revised by /distill)
- .spec/decisions/supervision_tree.md (already authored by /distill — do not rewrite)
- everything else in lib/mutagen_ex/, test/, priv/, etc.

Allowed touches:
- lib/mutagen_ex/application.ex (NEW)
- mix.exs (only the `mod:` line inside `application/0`)

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do not introduce additional supervised children — only the named Task.Supervisor for now.
- Do not change `extra_applications` in mix.exs.
- Do not add `MutagenEx.run/2` or any other public-API surface (Option B per the plan's Q1 resolution).
- Do not refactor or duplicate `.17`'s `with_restore/4`.

Merge gate:
- [ ] mix compile --warnings-as-errors green
- [ ] mix test green
- [ ] Fresh `iex -S mix` smoke: TaskSup + Supervisor whereis return pids
- [ ] TR-5 concrete check green (scope_resolver, scope_resolver_property, coverage_runner test files all pass with Application running)
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: agent-a83e8abf42dc7e70f (worker subagent of orchestrator session 81a13509-99b2-41c4-9907-6ad88ad2ede0)
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex-mutagen-wrd/81a13509-99b2-41c4-9907-6ad88ad2ede0/subagents/agent-a83e8abf42dc7e70f.jsonl

Commit: f384c25
Branch: mutagen-wrd.18.1/app-bootstrap (worktree /Users/mz/dev/mz/mutagen_ex-app-bootstrap, based on epic/mutagen-wrd @ 55af051)
Files touched:
- lib/mutagen_ex/application.ex (NEW, 56 lines, @moduledoc + start/2)
- mix.exs (one-line addition: `mod: {MutagenEx.Application, []}` inside application/0; extra_applications unchanged)
Tests added: none (S1 is structural; supervision_test.exs is explicitly out-of-scope for S1 — owned by S4 per ticket Out of Scope).
Verification stubs flipped: none (mutation_pipeline.v6 + coverage.v5 stay `execute: false` because they reference test/mutagen_ex/supervision_test.exs which S4 introduces; v1-v5 of mutation_pipeline and v1-v4 of coverage are already `execute: true` upstream).

Local sanity (run in stage worktree):
- `mix compile --warnings-as-errors`: green
- `mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_test.exs test/mutagen_ex/baseline_test.exs test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_raise_test.exs test/mutagen_ex/integration/c1_test.exs test/mutagen_ex/integration/c2_test.exs` (mutation_pipeline v1..v5): 39 tests, 0 failures.
- `mix test test/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner_test.exs test/mutagen_ex/ast_cache_test.exs` (coverage v1..v4): 22 tests, 0 failures.
- TR-5 concrete check `mix test test/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver_test.exs test/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver_property_test.exs test/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner_test.exs`: 38 tests, 0 failures.
- Full `mix test`: 357 tests, 0 failures (13 excluded).
- iex smoke (via `mix run --eval`): `{Process.whereis(MutagenEx.Supervisor) |> is_pid(), Process.whereis(MutagenEx.TaskSup) |> is_pid(), Application.ensure_all_started(:mutagen_ex)}` returned `{true, true, {:ok, []}}` — both supervisors are alive, application already started by Mix boot.

Scope check (mechanical): diff is exactly 2 files, both in Allowed touches. `lib/mutagen_ex/application.ex` is the NEW file the ticket calls for; `mix.exs` change is bounded to the `mod:` line as required.

Summary: Adds the OTP application callback and minimal supervision tree foundation (one-for-one MutagenEx.Supervisor wrapping a named MutagenEx.TaskSup Task.Supervisor) per .spec/decisions/supervision_tree.md. Closes F25 structurally: library callers depending on :mutagen_ex now get the supervision tree on application start without any new public-API surface. S2 (mutation_loop async_nolink swap) and S3 (coverage_runner singleton-ownership message enrichment) can now reference MutagenEx.TaskSup by name knowing it is alive at runtime.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: agent-aaf4d8a40b8f5cb07 (subagent of orchestrator session 81a13509-99b2-41c4-9907-6ad88ad2ede0)
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex-mutagen-wrd/81a13509-99b2-41c4-9907-6ad88ad2ede0/subagents/agent-aaf4d8a40b8f5cb07.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 2
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0
- mix.exs diff is bounded to the `mod:` line inside `application/0` (only the comma after `:logger` plus the new `mod:` line); `extra_applications` unchanged.
- `.spec/decisions/supervision_tree.md` is on disk and shows zero diff against the base SHA (unchanged by this commit, as required).

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run (execute: true subset, as required for S1):
  - mutation_pipeline.v1 (baseline_test.exs): 8 tests, 0 failures.
  - mutation_pipeline.v2 (mutation_runner_test.exs): 22 tests, 0 failures.
  - mutation_pipeline.v3 (integration/c1_test.exs): 1 test, 0 failures.
  - mutation_pipeline.v4 (integration/c2_test.exs): 1 test, 0 failures.
  - mutation_pipeline.v5 (mutation_runner_raise_test.exs): 7 tests, 0 failures.
  - coverage.v1 (coverage_runner_test.exs): 12 tests, 0 failures.
  - coverage.v2 (coverage_runner_test.exs --only scope_filter): 2 tests, 0 failures.
  - coverage.v3 (ast_cache_test.exs): 10 tests, 0 failures.
  - coverage.v4 (integration/c1_test.exs): 1 test, 0 failures.
  - mutation_pipeline.v6 and coverage.v5 stay `execute: false` (point at test/mutagen_ex/supervision_test.exs which is explicitly S4's deliverable per ticket Out of Scope). Correct for S1.
- Stubs flipped to true: 0 (none expected — S1 is structural, no new test files).
- TR-5 concrete check (scope_resolver + scope_resolver_property + coverage_runner): 38 tests, 0 failures with the Application running.
- Full `mix test`: 357 tests, 0 failures (13 excluded). Project verification green.
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors: green.
- Runtime behaviors (mix run smoke):
  `{Process.whereis(MutagenEx.Supervisor) |> is_pid(), Process.whereis(MutagenEx.TaskSup) |> is_pid(), Application.ensure_all_started(:mutagen_ex)}`
  returned `{true, true, {:ok, []}}`. Both supervisors are alive; the application was already started by Mix boot (empty started-list is the documented success shape).

Requirements coverage (one line per must requirement directly advanced by this S1 ticket — the structural requirements that S1 plants. r1..r12 of mutation_pipeline and r1..r7 of coverage are continuing requirements whose tests already exist and just re-passed with the Application running):
- mutagen.mutation_pipeline.r13 (Application/Supervisor/TaskSup topology + `mod:` declaration): structurally satisfied by lib/mutagen_ex/application.ex (use Application + Supervisor.start_link with `Task.Supervisor` child named `MutagenEx.TaskSup`, root named `MutagenEx.Supervisor`) and the new `mod: {MutagenEx.Application, []}` line in mix.exs. Runtime evidence: mix run smoke returned `{true, true, {:ok, []}}`. Per-test coverage is owned by mutagen.mutation_pipeline.v6 (test/mutagen_ex/supervision_test.exs, `execute: false`) which the ticket explicitly defers to S4. NOT COVERED by an existing test assertion in S1 — but this is by ticket design (S4 owns supervision_test.exs); the Merge gate replaces test-level coverage with the iex/mix-run smoke check, which passed.
- mutagen.mutation_pipeline.r14 (terminate_child propagates :shutdown through link tree, classes (a)/(b) descendants reaped): the named supervisor exists but the swap from `Task.async`/`Task.shutdown(:brutal_kill)` to `Task.Supervisor.async_nolink`/`terminate_child` is explicitly out-of-scope for S1 (owned by S2 per ticket Out of Scope; spec text and the application.ex moduledoc both say so). NOT COVERED here, by ticket design. S1's job is to plant the supervisor so S2 has something to call.
- mutagen.coverage.r8 (TaskSup as singleton owner of :cover_server + ExUnit.Server; rejection message names it): the supervisor exists, but the message-enrichment surface change in coverage_runner.ex is explicitly out-of-scope for S1 (owned by S3). NOT COVERED here, by ticket design.
- Continuing r-IDs in both subjects (mutation_pipeline.r1..r12 and coverage.r1..r7): covered by the v1..v5 / v1..v4 stubs that ran green above with the Application now booting on every test run — no regression.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none.
  - extra_applications unchanged ([:logger]).
  - No new external deps in mix.exs.
  - No new public-API surface (lib/mutagen_ex.ex does not exist; was deleted in .17).
  - No additional supervised children beyond MutagenEx.TaskSup.
  - No refactor of `with_restore/4` (lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex not in diff).
  - mutation_loop.ex / coverage_runner.ex unchanged — grep confirms `MutagenEx.TaskSup` is referenced only from the new application.ex.
- Project verification: pass (mix compile --warnings-as-errors + full mix test, 357/357).
- Audit-expanded scope (per step 11): none — no prior AUDIT: BOUNCE comments on this ticket.

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- The two new requirements (mutation_pipeline.r13, mutation_pipeline.r14, coverage.r8) whose contracts S1 partially satisfies do not yet have ExUnit assertions wired up — that is S4's deliverable (test/mutagen_ex/supervision_test.exs) and the spec verification stubs v6 / v5 deliberately stay `execute: false` until S4 lands. The ticket explicitly substitutes a Merge-gate iex/mix-run smoke for ExUnit coverage in S1; that smoke passed. Not a Gap because the ticket's contract is "plant the supervisor and let v1..v5 stay green", and that contract is met.
- The application.ex moduledoc cites `.spec/decisions/supervision_tree.md` and names F25/F2-arch/F26 consistent with the decision file's framing — no self-contradiction in the deliverable.
- Application.ensure_all_started/1 returns `{:ok, []}` (empty list) when the app is already started by Mix boot; the ticket text says `{:ok, _started}` which matches (the underscore-prefixed binding deliberately does not constrain the list contents).

AUDIT: APPROVE
Session: agent-ab84dd2be8f82babc (subagent of orchestrator session 81a13509-99b2-41c4-9907-6ad88ad2ede0)
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex-mutagen-wrd/81a13509-99b2-41c4-9907-6ad88ad2ede0/subagents/agent-ab84dd2be8f82babc.jsonl

Reasoning: Re-verified the diff and all verifier claims against the stage source tree.
Mechanical scope is exact: diff = lib/mutagen_ex/application.ex (NEW) + mix.exs (single
`mod:` line addition, extra_applications unchanged); both in Allowed touches; zero
out-of-scope globs touched; .spec/decisions/supervision_tree.md shows zero diff vs base
55af051 (decision file was authored at base SHA, untouched by f384c25). Intent-level
scope holds — no new supervised children beyond MutagenEx.TaskSup, no new public-API
surface (lib/mutagen_ex.ex absent as expected post-.17), no refactor of with_restore/4
(mutation_runner.ex not in diff), grep would confirm MutagenEx.TaskSup is referenced
only from the new application.ex.

The verifier's "NOT COVERED by ticket design" deferral for mutation_pipeline.r13/r14
and coverage.r8 is legitimate per the explicit ticket decomposition: the S1 ticket's
Out of Scope (files) section assigns mutation_loop.ex → S2, coverage_runner.ex → S3,
and supervision_test.exs → S4, and the parent epic mutagen-wrd.18 mirrors this
five-stage split. The S1 Merge gate intentionally substitutes the iex/mix-run smoke
("Fresh iex -S mix smoke: TaskSup + Supervisor whereis return pids") for ExUnit
coverage of r13's runtime invariant — not a deferral hidden in a Note but the
literal contract the ticket asks S1 to meet. r14's terminate_child swap and
coverage.r8's message enrichment are out-of-scope file edits for S1; S1 plants only
the supervisor those S2/S3 changes will reference. Verification stubs v6 (mutation
pipeline) and v5 (coverage) correctly remain execute: false because they point at
test/mutagen_ex/supervision_test.exs which S4 introduces.

Re-ran from stage worktree at f384c25: mix compile --warnings-as-errors green; v1..v5
of mutation_pipeline = 39 tests / 0 failures; v1..v4 of coverage = 22 tests / 0
failures; TR-5 concrete check (scope_resolver + scope_resolver_property +
coverage_runner) = 38 tests / 0 failures; mix run smoke returned `{true, true,
{:ok, []}}` — application booted, both named supervisors alive. Counts match the
verifier exactly. No miscount, no rubber-stamped stub, no scope violation, no
intent-level violation. The ticket's contract (plant the supervisor; keep v1..v5
green; prove runtime via smoke) is met cleanly.

title: S1: MutagenEx.Application + mix.exs mod: + decision file
